revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Ikagu-jinja]][[ja:伊香具神社]] {{Expand Japanese|伊香具神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Ikagu Shrine | native_name = 伊香具神社 | image = Ikagu-jinja, nakamon-1.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Ikagu Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35.50488|136.202093}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Ikatsuomikoto|lt=Ikatsuomikoto|ja|伊賀津臣命|qid=Q109599098|1=Igatsuomi no Mikoto}} }} {{nihongo|'''Ikagu Shrine'''|伊香具神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ōmi Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Q9610964|ltq=Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11638477|ltq=Q11638477|qid=Q11638477|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province}} ** Series ordinal: 67 ** P155: {{ill|Tsukubusuma Shrine|lt=Tsukubusuma Shrine|en|Tsukubusuma Shrine|fr|Sanctuaire Tsukubusuma|ja|都久夫須麻神社|qid=Q11643470|1=Tsukubusuma Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q135098105|ltq=Q135098105|qid=Q135098105|1=Nomino Shrine (Q135098105)}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q844130|ltq=Q844130|qid=Q844130|1=Nagahama, Shiga}} * {{ill|Ōmi Province|lt=Ōmi Province|ca|Província d'Ōmi|ckb|پارێزگای ئۆمی|cs|Provincie Ómi|de|Provinz Ōmi|en|Ōmi Province|es|Provincia de Ōmi|fa|ولایت اومی|fi|Ōmin provinssi|fr|Province d'Ōmi|gl|Provincia de Ōmi|hak|Ōmi-koet|id|Provinsi Ōmi|it|Ōmi|ja|近江国|ko|오미국|lt|Omio žemė|nl|Omi (provincie)|pl|Prowincja Ōmi|pt|Província de Ōmi|ru|Оми (провинция)|simple|Ōmi Province|sk|Ómi (provincia)|sr|Провинција Оми|tr|Ōmi (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Омі|vi|Tỉnh Ōmi|zh_min_nan|Ômi-kok|zh_yue|近江國|zh|近江國|qid=Q843882|1=Ōmi Province}} * {{ill|Q1042353|ltq=Q1042353|qid=Q1042353|1=Ika District, Shiga}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|35.50488|136.202093}} == image (P18) == * Ikagu-jinja, nakamon-1.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Q109599098|ltq=Q109599098|qid=Q109599098|1=Igatsuomi no Mikoto}} == described by source (P1343) == * {{ill|Q135037878|ltq=Q135037878|qid=Q135037878|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database (old)}} ** P2699: https://web.archive.org/web/20060725073022/http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp:80/db/jinja/210601.html == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181847 ** P3831: {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q90139226|ltq=Q90139226|qid=Q90139226|1=Ken-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == P14005 (P14005) == * {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|ko|종5위|zh|從五位|qid=Q11071125|1=Junior Fifth Rank}} * {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|ko|종4위|zh|從四位|qid=Q11071127|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} == architectural style (P149) == * {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|lt=nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|fr|Nagare-zukuri|it|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|pl|Nagare-zukuri|ru|Нагарэ-дзукури|sv|Nagare-zukuri|zh|流造|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * いかぐじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 9160005004501 == Commons category (P373) == * Ikagu-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県長浜市木之本町大音688', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Ikagu-jinja}} [[JA:伊香具神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11381016}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Ikagu Shrine |Image = [[File:Ikagu-jinja, nakamon-1.jpg|270px]]
Middle Gate |Location = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|qid=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagahama City|Q844130|qid=Q844130|1=Nagahama, Shiga}} Kinoshimo Town, Oto 688 |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|30|16.2|N|136|12|07.6|E|region:JP-25_scale:20000_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Ikatsumi-no-Mikoto |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|lt=Myōjin Taisha|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Founded = (Traditionally) before the 10th year of the Hakuhō era during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|qid=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}{{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|qid=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|lt=one-bay Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternative name = |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=April 6|Q2506|qid=Q2506|1=April 6}} |Rituals = {{ill|lt=Okonai|Q11291908|qid=Q11291908|1=Okonai}} ({{ill|lt=February 24|Q2355|qid=Q2355|1=February 24}}) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Ikagu Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Ikagu Shrine''' (いかぐじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kinoshimo Town, Oto, {{ill|lt=Nagahama City|Q844130|qid=Q844130|1=Nagahama, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|qid=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. Its shrine crest is the “Upper Wisteria.” It is称ed one of the most prominent shrines in the Kohoku region. The {{ill|lt=Okonai|Q11291908|qid=Q11291908|1=Okonai}} ritual is held on February 24. Its torii gate is of a unique style known as the Ikagu-style torii, the only one of its kind in Japan. It is said to resemble a combination of the great torii of {{ill|lt=Itsukushima Shrine|Q191763|qid=Q191763|1=Itsukushima Shrine}} and the triple torii of {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542|qid=Q705542|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}}. === Enshrined Deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ikatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} This deity is said to be identical with “Ikato-mi,” who appears in the hagoromo (feather robe) legend found in fragments of the {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province Fudoki|Q11638480|qid=Q11638480|1=Ōmi no Kuni Fudoki}}, and is believed to possess divine virtues that bring abundant harvests. === History === In ancient times, when the area was still an undeveloped marshland, the enshrined deity is said to have come and reclaimed the land, later enshrining himself here to protect his descendants (according to the {{ill|lt=Ōmi Yochi Shiryaku|Q85883966|qid=Q85883966|1=Ōmi Yochi Shiryaku}}). It is also said that before the 10th year of the Hakuhō era during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|qid=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}, a descendant named Ikano Sukune Toyoatsu built the shrine buildings (according to the “Shrine Origin Record”Nishikawa, cited in “Ikagu Shrine.”). In the first year of the {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} era ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|qid=Q23897|1=859}}), the shrine was promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade, Merit Rank Eight to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower GradeEntry of the 27th day of the first month of the same year in the {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|qid=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}., and in the 8th year it was further promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Upper GradeEntry of the intercalary 3rd month, 7th day of the same year in the Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku.. Under the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Engi system|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it was listed as a Myōjin Taisha. According to shrine tradition, in the 7th year of {{ill|lt=Kanpyō|Q1346292|qid=Q1346292|1=Kanpyō (era)}} ({{ill|lt=895|Q31119|qid=Q31119|1=895}}), {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|qid=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} personally copied the {{ill|lt=Lotus Sutra|Q861212|qid=Q861212|1=Lotus Sutra}} and the {{ill|lt=Golden Light Sutra|Q391694|qid=Q391694|1=Golden Light Sutra}} and dedicated them to the shrine, at which time an imperial plaque bearing the title “Shōichi-i Kun Itto Taisha Daimyōjin Kongōkaku-in Bosatsu” was bestowedNishikawa, “Ikagu Shrine.”. Later, {{ill|lt=Ashikaga Takauji|Q297107|qid=Q297107|1=Ashikaga Takauji}} donated 200 {{ill|lt=koku|Q855048|qid=Q855048|lt=koku|1=Koku}} of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine land|1=UNKNOWN}} and requested that prayers be conducted on the 18th day of the first, fifth, and ninth months. The {{ill|lt=Azai clan|Q1872031|qid=Q1872031|1=Azai clan}} also extended its patronage. However, during the warfare of the {{ill|lt=Battle of Shizugatake|Q225562|qid=Q225562|1=Battle of Shizugatake}}, the shrine buildings and ancient records were destroyed by fire, and the shrine lands were confiscated. In the 8th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1875|Q7764|qid=Q7764|1=1875}}), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, promoted to Prefectural Shrine in the 32nd year, and in the 40th year designated a shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and silk from the government. === Shrine Grounds === File:Ikagu-jinjam nakamon-2.jpg|Main hall and Middle Gate File:Ikagu-jinja, torii-2.jpg|Ikagu-style torii File:Ikagu-jinja, entrance.jpg|Shrine entrance File:Ikagu-jinja, otorii.jpg|Great torii === Subsidiary Shrines === '''Within the grounds''' * Okunomiya * Sannomiya * Hakusan Shrine '''Outside the grounds''' * Ota Shrine – a Shikinaisha. === Festivals === * Okonai (February 24) * Annual festival (April 6) === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === References === * Takeo Nishikawa, “Ikagu Shrine” (in *Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Sites*, Vol. 5 [New Edition Reprint], {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|qid=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}, published 2000) === External Links === {{Commonscat|Ikagu-jinja}} * [Ikagu Shrine] – Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines * [Ikagu Shrine] – Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources Collection” {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ikagu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ikagu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ikagu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nagahama, Shiga]] [[Category:長浜市の歴史]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:近江国の式内社|名いかくしんしや]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]]
